stemHtml: The French Safety Board (BEA) published a preliminary report of a serious incident involving an Airbus A330 being operated as a commercial air transport flight at Sydney, Australia. The Report states: "During take-off, a section of engine cowling from the NO. 1 engine separated from the aircraft. The crew conducted a fuel dump and returned the aircraft to Sydney." The incident did NOT involve an engine fire. Why did the crew dump fuel after take-off and at what minimum altitude it was done?​

options.a: They had to reduce landing weight and conducted a fuel dump minimum altitude of 6000 ft.
options.b: They had to reduce landing weight and conducted a fuel dump minimum altitude of 8000 ft.
options.c: They had to reduce landing weight and conducted a fuel dump minimum altitude of 4000 ft.
options.d: They had to reduce landing weight and conducted a fuel dump minimum altitude of 12 000 ft.
